title: FINRA Case 25-01584 — Adam Quarello v. Oppenheimer & Co., Inc., UBS Financial Services Inc
text:
Award
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
In the Matter of the Arbitration Between:
Claimant Case Number: 25-01584
Adam Manna Quarello
vs.
Respondent Hearing Site: Los Angeles, California
Oppenheimer & Co. Inc.
UBS Financial Services Inc.
Awards are rendered by independent arbitrators who are appointed pursuant to Rule 13806 of
the Code of Arbitration Procedure (“Code”) to issue final, binding decisions. FINRA makes
available an arbitration forum—pursuant to rules approved by the SEC—but has no part in
deciding the award.
Nature of the Dispute: Associated Person vs. Members
The expungement hearing was conducted by videoconference.
REPRESENTATION OF PARTIES
For Claimant Adam Manna Quarello (“Claimant”): William Bean, Esq., HLBS Law, Broomfield,
Colorado.
For Respondent Oppenheimer & Co. Inc. (“Oppenheimer”): Alexa P. Saberito, Esq.,
Oppenheimer & Co. Inc., New York, New York.
For Respondent UBS Financial Services Inc. (“UBS”): John Murphy, Esq., John Murphy &
Associates, P.C., New York, New York.
CASE INFORMATION
Statement of Claim filed on or about: August 1, 2025.
Claimant signed the Submission Agreement: August 1, 2025.
Statement of Answer filed by Oppenheimer on or about: October 28, 2025.
Oppenheimer signed the Submission Agreement: October 20, 2025.
Statement of Answer filed by UBS on or about: September 30, 2025
UBS signed the Submission Agreement: September 30, 2025.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01584
Award Page 2 of 7
CASE SUMMARY
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant asserted a claim seeking expungement of customer dispute
information from registration records maintained by the Central Registration Depository (“CRD”).
In the Statement of Answer, Oppenheimer did not oppose Claimant’s expungement request.
In the Statement of Answer, UBS did not oppose Claimant’s expungement request.
RELIEF REQUESTED
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant (CRD Number 4461542) requested:
1. Expungement of the Occurrences, customer dispute Occurrence numbers: 1429680,
1617578, 1893416, 1951300, 2208580, and 2306684, from his CRD records, pursuant to
FINRA Rule 2080(b)(1)(A), as the claim, allegation, or information is factually impossible
or clearly erroneous;
2. Expungement of the Occurrences, customer dispute Occurrence numbers: 1429680,
1617578, 1893416, 1951300, 2208580, and 2306684, from his CRD records, pursuant to
FINRA Rule 2080(b)(1)(C), as the claim, allegation, or information is false;
3. Deletion of all Disclosure Reporting Pages accompanying the Occurrences, customer
dispute Occurrence numbers: 1429680, 1617578, 1893416, 1951300, 2208580, and
2306684; and
4. Any and all other relief that the Arbitrator deems just and equitable.
In the Statement of Answer, Oppenheimer did not set forth any specific relief request.
In the Statement of Answer, UBS requested that all costs and fees associated with the Claim be
assessed solely against Claimant.
OTHER ISSUES CONSIDERED AND DECIDED
The Arbitrators acknowledge that they have each read the pleadings and other materials filed by
the parties.
On August 4, 2025, Claimant advised that the customers in Occurrence Numbers 1429680,
1617578, 1893416, 1951300, 2208580, and 2306684 (“Customers”) were served with a copy of
the Statement of Claim within 10 days of filing the Statement of Claim and provided proof of
service.
On October 1, 2025, Claimant advised that the Customers were served with a copy of the UBS’s
Statement of Answer within 10 days of its filing and provided proof of service.
On October 28, 2025, Claimant advised that the Customers were served with a copy of the
Oppenheimer’s Statement of Answer within 10 days of its filing and provided proof of service.
On February 19, 2026, the authorized representative of the state securities regulator for California
submitted a written statement and opposed the expungement request.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01584
Award Page 3 of 7
The Panel conducted a recorded hearing by videoconference on March 10, 2026, so the parties
could present oral argument and evidence on Claimant’s request for expungement. Claimant
appeared by videoconference at the expungement hearing. Oppenheimer appeared at the
expungement hearing and as stated in the Statement of Answer, did not oppose the request for
expungement. UBS did not appear at the hearing.
The Customers did not appear at the expungement hearing.
The authorized representatives of the state securities regulator for California appeared at the
expungement hearing by videoconference and opposed the expungement request.
The Panel noted Claimant’s representation that a prior arbitration panel or court has not
previously ruled on expungement of the same occurrences in the CRD.
The Panel reviewed the settlement documentation related to Occurrence Number 1951300,
considered the amount of payment made to any party to the settlement, and considered other
relevant terms and conditions of the settlement. The Panel noted that the settlement was not
conditioned on any party to the settlement not opposing the expungement request and that
Claimant did not contribute to the settlement amount.
The Panel noted that the disputes related to Occurrence Numbers 1429680, 1617578, 1893416,
2208580, and 2306684 were not settled. Therefore, there was no settlement documentation to
review.
The Panel relied upon the following documentary or other evidence: Claimant’s exhibits and
testimony of Adam Manna Quarello.
AWARD
After considering the pleadings, the testimony and evidence presented at the hearing, and any
post-hearing submissions, the Panel has decided in full and final resolution of the issues
submitted for determination as follows:
1. The Panel awards the expungement of all references to Occurrence Numbers 1429680,
1617578, 1893416, 1951300, 2208580, and 2306684 from registration records maintained
by the CRD for Adam Manna Quarello (CRD Number 4461542) with the understanding that,
pursuant to Rule 2080, Adam Manna Quarello must obtain confirmation from a court of
competent jurisdiction before the CRD will execute the expungement directive.
Unless specifically waived in writing by FINRA, parties seeking judicial confirmation of an
arbitration award containing customer dispute expungement relief must name FINRA as an
additional party and serve FINRA with all appropriate documents.
Pursuant to Rule 13805 of the Code, the Panel has made the following affirmative finding of
fact:
The claim, allegation, or information is false.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01584
Award Page 4 of 7
The Panel has made the above finding based on the following reasons:
The Panel rejects California's position that Occurrences numbers 1429680,
1617578, 1893416 and 1951300 are untimely under FINRA Rule 13206 (a). The
Panel found these Occurrences continuous and on going and eligible for
expungement.
The Panel grants expungement of Occurrences 1429680, 1617578, 1893416,
19551300, 22089580, and 23006684 pursuant to FINRA Rule 2080(b)(1)(C) as the
claims, allegations, or information is false.
Occurrence Number 1429680- Customer ME
The testimony of Mr. Quarello along with the exhibits disclosed that Customer ME
was a sophisticated investor and had a substantial net worth. Mr. Quarello had
done due diligence on the Wave stock and given his research to Customer ME.
Customer ME knew that Wave was a speculative investment. Knowing the risk in
investing in Wave, Customer ME purchased it several times. UBS investigated Customer
ME’s claim and found it lacked merit.
Occurrence 1617578- Customer SF
The testimony and exhibits showed that Customer SF had inherited millions of
dollars and had a net worth of over 50 million dollars. She had big positions in
Google and Facebook which she was trading options. Customer SF had signed
documents authorizing the option trading. Customer SF was doing trading with Mr.
J not with Mr. Quarello. Oppenheimer investigated Customer SF’s claim that she
was pressured into trading options and found it lacked merit. Customer SF did not
pursue her claim.
Occurrence 1893416- Customer MF
Full disclosure of the costs and risks of Customer MF’s annuity was made to her.
Customer MF was given a 30-day look back to terminate the investment. Customer MF
did not file her complaint of unsuitable investment until 2 years after she made
the investment. Mr. Quarello met with Customer MF several times before she
purchased the annuity explaining its terms.
Occurrence Number 1951300- Customer DL
Customer DL was a sophisticated investor as well a successful businesswoman
running an interior decorating business. She had recently divorced and wanted
growth for her investments. Customer DL used her investments to sustain her high
lifestyle using both income and principal to meet her needs. Customer DL was warned by
her financial team as well as her CPA that she had to reduce her spending. As her
spending continued, she was forced to liquidate positions to meet her financial
obligations. Customer DL filed an arbitration case against Oppenheimer. Mr. Quarello
was not a party to the suit, nor did he contribute to the settlement. The Panel found that

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01584
Award Page 5 of 7
Mr. Quarello did not breach any fiduciary duty to Customer DL nor was his conduct
wrong. The Panel found there were no misrepresentations or omissions.
Occurrence Number 220880- Customer CF
The Panel found that the 12 ETFs owned by Customer CF were appropriate and
diversified. After hearing testimony from Mr. Quarello, California withdrew its
objection to expungement of this Occurrence Number. Customer CF withdrew her
complaint and did not pursue any claim in arbitration.
Occurrence Number 2306684- Customer JS
Testimony from Mr. Quarello disclosed Customer JS was a sophisticated investor
with a high income. His net worth was over 2.5 million dollars, and he had been
investing for years. Customer JS reviewed his portfolio daily and was fully aware of his
financial positions. During the time Customer JS was a client of Mr. Quarello, his net
worth increased $200,000.00. Oppenheimer did its own inquiry of Customer JS ‘s
allegations of unauthorized trading and found it lacked merit. The Panel found that at all
times Customer JS knew what he was investing in and what positions he held.
2. Any and all claims for relief not specifically addressed herein are denied.
FEES
Pursuant to the Code, the following fees are assessed:
Filing Fees
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services assessed a filing fee* for each claim:
Initial Claim Filing Fee =$ 2,000.00
*The filing fee is made up of a non-refundable and a refundable portion.
Member Fees
Member fees are assessed to each member firm that is a party in these proceedings or to the
member firm(s) that employed the associated person(s) at the time of the event(s) giving rise to
the dispute. Accordingly, as parties, Respondents Oppenheimer and UBS are each assessed
the following:
Member Surcharge =$ 2,600.00
Member Process Fee =$ 5,005.00
Hearing Session Fees and Assessments
The Panel has assessed hearing session fees for each session conducted. A session is any
meeting between the parties and the Arbitrator(s), including a pre-hearing conference with the
Arbitrator(s), which lasts four (4) hours or less. Fees associated with these proceedings are:

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01584
Award Page 6 of 7
One (1) pre-hearing session with the Panel @ $1,725.00/session =$ 1,725.00
Pre-Hearing Conference: December 10, 2025 1 session
One (1) hearing session @ $1,725.00/session =$ 1,725.00
Hearing: March 10, 2026 1 session
Total Hearing Session Fees =$ 3,450.00
The Panel has assessed the total hearing session fees to Claimant.
All balances are payable to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services and are due upon receipt.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01584
Award Page 7 of 7
ARBITRATION PANEL
Steven H. Vogel - Public Arbitrator, Presiding Chairperson
Steven N. Ainbinder - Public Arbitrator
Joseph J. Dougherty - Public Arbitrator
I, the undersigned Arbitrator, do hereby affirm that I am the individual described herein and who
executed this instrument, which is my award.
Concurring Arbitrators' Signatures
Steven H. Vogel 03/20/2026
Steven H. Vogel Signature Date
Public Arbitrator, Presiding Chairperson

Steven N. Ainbinder Signature Date

Joseph J. Dougherty Signature Date
Public Arbitrator
Awards are rendered by independent arbitrators who are chosen by the parties to issue final,
binding decisions. FINRA makes available an arbitration forum—pursuant to rules approved by
the SEC—but has no part in deciding the award.
March 23, 2026
Date of Service (For FINRA Dispute Resolution Services use only)
respondent: Oppenheimer & Co., Inc., UBS Financial Services Inc
